formations_blocks: Argentina controlling possession at a low tempo
Algeria growing into midfield possession after the disallowed goal
key_matchups: Chaibi between the fullback and centre-half vs Martinez
phases.summary: Argentina started in a 4-3-3 without the ball and were content to dominate possession slowly, inviting Algeria to step out rather than forcing play. Algeria’s best spell came from a slick midfield recycle and a run between the lines that produced a disallowed goal for offside, after which they looked more confident in midfield despite Argentina still controlling the rhythm.

adjustments: Argentina increasingly focused on vertical passes through the lines
Messi dropping deep to receive possession
Algeria retreating into a low block after conceding
phases.summary: Argentina controlled the phase with an extremely compact build-up, circulating the ball in tight spaces before punching vertical passes through the lines. Messi dropped deep to link play, then found pockets between the lines to score, while Algeria responded by sinking into a deep low block and refusing to press. The overall story is Argentina's technical superiority in cramped areas versus Algeria's increasingly passive defensive shell.

key_matchups: Lionel Messi vs Algeria's defensive efforts (Argentina's best player, match dominated by Messi)
phases.summary: The half-time note presents Argentina leading 1-0, with the match described as being dominated by Lionel Messi. The only clear tactical takeaway in the text is Messi’s central importance to Argentina’s play, while no specific formations, pressing structures, or in-game shape changes are explicitly mentioned.

formations_blocks: Argentina press/man-mark in midfield with Mac Allister and Fernandez closing Maza
Argentina counter-attacking shape with repeated phases through the right side
adjustments: Argentina changed right back at half-time: Molina on for Montiel
Argentina made attacking substitutions: Alvarez and Gonzalez on for Almada and La Martinez
Algeria continued to build through Maza between the lines and tried to bypass the press with switches and combinations
phases.summary: Argentina’s half-time change at right back was followed by a period in which Algeria tried to settle into possession, using switches and Maza between the lines, but Argentina’s midfield and defensive screen repeatedly shut the door. Mac Allister’s man-marking of Maza, backed up by Fernandez and Li Martinez stepping in aggressively, limited Algeria’s threat, while Argentina remained dangerous in transition and continued to create right-side chances before Messi eventually doubled the lead from the 60th minute.

formations_blocks: low-intensity possession with Algeria circulating the ball outside the box
adjustments: Argentina consolidate after the third goal and are comfortable conceding harmless possession outside the area
Messi and Romero are substituted off; Otamendi and Nico Paz come on
Algeria make late substitutions with Zerrouki and Boulbina
key_matchups: Messi vs Algeria's back line and Zidane on the 76' goal
Mahrez vs Argentina's defensive wall / back four on the edge-of-box free-kick
phases.summary: Argentina's third goal effectively ends the contest, with Messi orchestrating a rapid break and then finishing to seal a dominant lead. After that, Argentina drop into a disciplined defensive shape, allowing Algeria sterile possession outside the box while the tempo falls sharply and the game becomes pure garbage time.
